description: Non-conformant label-309 value shapes and their pinned dispositions. CHUNK_TOO_LARGE is emitted by the pre-validator reassembly step; every other shape here is MALFORMED_CBOR.

vectors.name: bare-map
vectors.note: The record map stored directly under label 309 (no chunk array) is rejected; the chunk-array form is required regardless of body length.

vectors.name: bare-byte-string
vectors.note: A single bare byte string is not the chunk array.

vectors.name: text-string-element
vectors.note: Array elements must be byte strings.

vectors.name: integer-element
vectors.note: Array elements must be byte strings.

vectors.name: nested-array-element
vectors.note: Array elements must be byte strings.

vectors.name: indefinite-length-bstr-element
vectors.note: An indefinite-length byte-string element is rejected.

vectors.name: chunk-65-bytes
vectors.note: A 65-byte element exceeds the per-chunk 64-byte ceiling — the single carriage-level size rule. Such a value cannot appear on chain (nodes refuse it at submission), but an implementation validating caller-supplied bytes MUST still pin this rejection.

vectors.name: empty-array-empty-body
vectors.note: An empty transport array reassembles to zero bytes; the code surfaces from the canonical decode of the empty record body, not from the transport layer.

vectors.name: zero-only-chunks-empty-body
vectors.note: Only zero-length chunks: tolerated at the transport layer, but the reassembled empty body fails canonical decode.
